article_title: OCaml 5.5.0 Reaches Release Candidate With Modular Explicits and a Fully Relocatable Compiler

content_preview.title: OCaml 5.5.0 Reaches Release Candidate With Modular Explicits and a Fully Relocatable Compiler
content_preview.summary: OCaml 5.5.0-rc1 lands modular explicits, the inference-free half of the long-sought modular implicits, plus a compiler that can be moved between directories.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The OCaml team has tagged the first release candidate for OCaml 5.5.0, signaling that the next version of the functional language is nearly ready to ship. According to the [ocaml/ocaml tags page](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/tags), the `5.5.0-rc1` tag was published on June 10, 2026, following a `5.5.0-beta1` tag on April 20 and a first alpha back on February 25.

The headline feature of the release is modular explicits, a language addition that has been discussed in the OCaml comm...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (601 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis structure is appropriate. Technical depth is accurate without overstatement.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk and SHA256-verified against manifest (uncompressed hashes match exactly). source-0.html.gz (github.com/ocaml/ocaml/tags, 200): confirms 5.5.0-rc1 tagged Jun 10 2026, 5.5.0-beta1 Apr 20 2026, 5.5.0-alpha1 Feb 25 2026, with three alphas (alpha1/2/3) and one beta — all dates and cadence claims match. source-2.html.gz (raw.githubusercontent.com Changes, 200): confirms verbatim the modular explicits type entry '(module M : S) -> t[M] ... module-dependent functions (also called: modular explicits)', the relocatable compiler line 'The compiler is now relocatable: it can be copied/moved to a different directory and everything still works', CVE-2026-41083 (Filename.quote_command on Windows) and CVE-2026-28364 (robustify intern.c) both verbatim, plus new String functions and Lazy.Mutexed. source-1.html.gz (github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/13275, 200): all four direct quotes ('Modular explicits are modular implicits, without this implicit inference part -- the hard part, in many respects', 'They are not a replacement...', 'There is now a consensus...', 'reuse the syntax of first-class module...') appear verbatim. All three URLs are allowlisted GitHub primaries (github.com, raw.githubusercontent.com); no off-allowlist domains.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (version tags, dates, CVE IDs, type signature, quotes) traces to a cited snapshot. The two CVE IDs (CVE-2026-41083, CVE-2026-28364) appear verbatim in the Changes changelog. Feature/CVE/date specifics are anchored to the official Changes changelog, the GitHub tags page, and PR #13275, satisfying Rule 9.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism; the 'What We Don't Know' and 'Analysis' sections appropriately hedge on RC-vs-stable and on the open modular-implicits timeline. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: No prior OCaml coverage in the archive. Adjacent programming-language articles (Python/Django, Pyrefly, GCC 16.1, KernelScript) cover different languages/tools — no duplication or overlap.
editor_notes.framing_check: CRITICAL RC-vs-stable framing is correct. Headline says 'Reaches Release Candidate'; the body consistently treats 5.5.0-rc1 as a release candidate, not a final stable release: 'tagged the first release candidate', 'nearly ready to ship', 'essentially feature-frozen and undergoing final stabilization', and explicitly 'As a release candidate, 5.5.0-rc1 is a final stabilization step rather than a finished release, and candidates can slip if regressions surface before the stable tag.' No stable-release misclassification. Confirmed the prior off-allowlist sources were dropped: no discuss.ocaml.org or ocaml.org citation remains, and the removed details (opam-install line, 'December 2025'/'opam clone switches') are absent. 'OCaml' and 'modular explicits' are spelled verbatim throughout (Rule 7).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-sourced News submission. Integrity checks pass (valid Ed25519 signature, matching payload hash). All claims, quotes, dates, version tags, and both CVE IDs verified verbatim against allowlisted GitHub primary snapshots. RC-vs-stable framing is accurate. APPROVE.
